explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 66 from a base nutrition score of 66. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: complete protein delivery is reliable
topPros.explanation: The category is supposed to do this well.

topPros.title: high satiety protein source
topPros.explanation: That is a major real-world strength for meat.

topPros.title: easy to centre a meal around

topCons.title: fat quality can go sideways fast
topCons.explanation: Some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up.

topCons.title: quality swings a lot by cut and processing
topCons.explanation: The category can look very different depending on what version you actually buy.

topCons.title: sourcing quality can change the real-world result a lot
topCons.explanation: Not every version of the same meat is equal.

closing.summary: Lamb does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
